PREFACE.

READER! (-Courteous,” “Intelligent,” “Patient,” and

“Worshipful,” as readers were addressed by authors
whilom), presuming you to possess the estimable qualities
implied by such designations, I have less timidity in asking
your indulgence while travelling with me through the following
chapters on weird and fanciful dclusions.

Possibly your patience will be tried by predilections of my
own for particular subjects, which may not prove so attractive
to you as they have bcen pleasant to myself. It is, also, morc
than rrobable that there are omissions, which have been rendered
inevitable by the confined compass of this book. But I trust
for your forbearancc, inasmuch as I have, perhaps, spared you
a long and wcarisome perusal of timc-worn books on occult
lore, that may have long slumbered, neglected, on thcir shelves,
and the study of which may not be recreative to general
readers.

It may be askcd, Of what use is the exposure of thc dark sidc
of human naturc,—thc weaknesses and follics of mankind?
Wcre these matters of the past only, we might be contented
with smiling at the ignorancc and fatuity of our ancestors, but
the inheritance of many strange and flimsy fancies has descended
to us through long ages, and is still decply engrafted on the
